digest.top_line: ENZARAD: MFS primary negative (HR 0.88, p=0.34); enzalutamide benefit isolated to cN1 (HR 0.43) and planned-pelvic-RT (HR 0.47) subgroups, hypothesis-generating.
digest.tldr: Prostate carries the day's only data: ENZARAD's 24-mo enzalutamide added to EBRT+ADT misses its MFS primary (HR 0.88, p=0.34), but the signal concentrates in cN1 (MFS HR 0.43) and planned-pelvic-RT (HR 0.47) subgroups, both risk-confounded and hypothesis-generating.

sites.intro: Lone reported trial: a negative MFS primary with an RT-subgroup signal that risk-confounding can fully explain.

studies.name: ENZARAD (ANZUP 1303)
studies.tldr: Primary MFS endpoint negative (HR 0.88, p=0.34); enzalutamide benefit concentrated in cN1 (HR 0.43) and planned-pelvic-RT (HR 0.47) subgroups.
details: 📊 OS NS: 8-yr 83% vs 80%, HR 0.87 (0.63-1.20), p=0.40 (OS was original 1° EP, switched to MFS after fewer deaths than expected)
🔍 International phase III, N=802 (8 countries), median f/u 8yr
💊 Exp = enzalutamide 160mg ×24mo; control = conventional NSAA ×6mo; both +24mo LHRH +EBRT
🔍 RT: prostate 78Gy or 46Gy+brachy boost; pelvic nodes required for cN1 (46Gy elective +boost to gross nodes), optional/pre-declared for cN0

details.text: 🔗 Weaker all-pt effect vs STAMPEDE abiraterone tracks more favorable ENZARAD risk profile

details: ⚠️ Pelvic-RT subgroup confounded by risk: that group 28% N1 / 62% Gleason 9-10 vs 0% N1 / 49% Gleason 9-10 in no-pelvic-RT pts
⚠️ Subgroups preplanned but hypothesis-generating; p-values nominal, no multiplicity adjustment, wide CIs

verdict.rationale: Randomized phase III, prespecified MFS primary endpoint negative (p=0.34); supports not adding enzalutamide for most high-risk localized pts. cN1/pelvic-RT subgroup benefit preplanned but hypothesis-generating.

studies.significance: The actionable split is by RT field: enzalutamide's benefit concentrates in cN1 (MFS HR 0.43) and planned-pelvic-RT (HR 0.47) pts, near-absent otherwise. But the pelvic-RT subgroup carried far higher baseline risk (28% N1 vs 0%), so nodal coverage and risk are confounded. Moves the cN1/pelvic-RT intensification decision, not cN0.
open_questions: Which pelvic-RT subgroups drive the enzalutamide benefit
Biomarkers to select patients for treatment intensification
Whether pelvic nodal RT is required for enzalutamide's distant-met benefit

related_trials.brief_title: Reduced Length of ADT and ARTA With XRT in High-Risk Prostate Cancer (RELAX): A Randomised Trial
related_trials.brief_summary: Aim of the study to improve the treatment of high-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer by comparing reduced duration of hormone therapy to the standard duration of two years, when combined with curative intent standard of care radiotherapy to prostate and pelvic nodes. The reduced duration will be intensified with addition of newer hormonal agents such as abiraterone or enzalutamide tablets, which have been shown to improve disease control in locally advanced prostate cancer. This is hoped to…

interventions.name: ADT for 9 months and ARTA for 6 months

related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:-

* Patients with biopsy proven prostate adenocarcinoma, deemed suitable for curative- intent radiotherapy
* Clinicoradiological stage T1-T4N0M0 using PSMA-PETCT and MRI
* High risk or very high risk (as per NCCN 2023): stage T3a + OR Gleason Score 4+4 or 4+5 OR PSA 20 +

related_trials.answers_question: Which pelvic-RT subgroups drive the enzalutamide benefit

related_trials.brief_title: Genomics in Michigan to AdJust Outcomes in Prostate canceR (G-MAJOR) for Men With Newly Diagnosed Favorable Risk Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This study seeks to determine the clinical impact of Gene Expression Classifier (GEC) testing in prostate cancer care while also developing a pragmatic approach for improved GEC clinical use and future study.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Prostate cancer patients who have undergone diagnostic prostate biopsy in the past 9 months.
* Prostate biopsy tumor tissue (FFPR block) available for processing
* Age 18 years or older
* PSA \<20 ng/ml
* Grade Group (GG) 1 cancer with \> 2 biopsy cores involved with cancer…

papers.title: ESMO 2025: Randomized Phase III Trial of Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) with Radiation Therapy with or without Enzalutamide for High Risk, Clinically Localized Prostate Cancer: ENZARAD (ANZUP 1303)
papers.abstract: ESMO 2025 ENZARAD (ANZUP 1303), randomized phase II trial of ADT + radiation therapy +/- enzalutamide, localized prostate cancer.
